title: Nashik family outing turns violent; nine detained
content: Nine individuals detained after a violent altercation during a Nashik family outing near Bhavli Dam waterfall

title: Police’s illegal entry into woman's bedroom amounts to violation of privacy: Bombay Hign Court
content: The bench observed that while monetary compensation cannot fully redress the invasion of privacy and dignity, it would provide some measure of solace and serve as a reminder that investigative powers must be exercised in accordance with law

title: How new tehsils, districts for Ladakh address political aspirations? | Explained
content: Why is the Centre rolling out new measures to Ladakh? What is LAHDC Act? What is the need for new districts and tehsils?

title: Amid wrangle, LDF lays groundwork for CPI-CPI(M) detente over Kerala Deputy Opposition Leader post
content: T.P. Ramakrishnan, who held a brief closed-door meeting with the CPI State Secretary Binoy Viswam, signalled that the rapprochement was not immediately at hand. He said the discussions were in their nascent stages and denied any rift in the coalition. Viswam merely stated that “CPI and CPI(M) pursued the same politics”

title: No more Chemburs: How can Indian cities ensure their trees do not ‘turn against’ their people
content: Even as the verdict of the inquiry committee in Chembur tree fall incident comes in, a stroll through three other Indian metros, Chennai, Bengaluru and Delhi, to find out if they are proactive enough with their trees to prevent a Chembur on their soil

title: Is the undocumented migrant counted?
content: Contrary to the claims that there could be 20 million “illegal Bangladeshi migrants” staying in India, an analysis based on Census data suggests that there is no surge of undocumented migrants from Bangladesh, at least between 1991 and 2011

title: CM Naidu calls for reforms in business rules, faster governance through technology
content: Naidu instructs officials to constitute a committee to redraft the Business Rules and introduce reforms that would make governance more efficient and responsive

title: West Bengal govt allocates 1,000 km of land to set up fence of about 172 km along international border
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that his government has expedited the land transfer process to the Border Security Force (BSF) for the construction of essential fencing.

title: Norway firm to set up India’s first river kinetic energy demo plant in Arunachal
content: Science and Technology Minister Dasanglu Pul says the technology to generate electricity from river currents without requiring major civil infrastructure could ensure environmentally responsible utilisation of natural resources

title: Ram Temple corruption episode biggest proof of BJP govt. failure: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai
content: ‘When it comes to taking credit for the temple, Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.P. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath are always at the forefront; hence they will be blamed for this heinous crime and theft at the temple,’ said Mr. Rai

title: Dr. Mustafa Kamal, the Abdullah who rooted for J&K’s pre-1953 position, dies in Srinagar
content: Mustafa Kamal would blame the Congress for embroiling the Kashmir issue by arresting his father and party founder Sheikh Muhammad Abdullah in 1953

title: India blames Pakistan for protests in PoK, seeks accountability
content: “We expect and hope that the international community will hold Pakistan fully accountable for egregious abuses and misdeeds,” says MEA spokesperson

title: Supreme Court says call records alone not cogent proof, clears woman of husband’s murder
content: Mere production of telephone records does not substitute substantive proof of an illicit affair leading to murder, the top court said in the case dating back to 2007

title: Can’t English be considered an indigenous Indian language, asks Supreme Court
content: SC questions the use of the colonially loaded term ‘native’ in CBSE circular, says the push for three-language scheme may be in the spirit of the Constitutional goal to use Indian languages for official purposes

title: Former SCERT Director arrested over errors in Odisha textbooks
content: Acts and omissions resulted in the publication and distribution of erroneous textbooks, causing wrongful loss to the State exchequer of approximately ₹175 crore, says Crime Branch

title: CJP protest Day 25: As Sonam Wangchuk’s condition deteriorates, leaders urge him to end hunger strike
content: Over 1,800 “writers, academics, social activists and other concerned citizens” signed on to an online petition asking Mr. Wangchuk to end his fast.

title: Odisha panel clears early release of prime convict in murder of Graham Staines
content: Odisha State Sentence Review Board has recommended Dara Singh’s release on the grounds of ‘good behaviour’

title: Migrant worker from Bihar dies in boiler explosion at factory in Gummidipoondi
content: Three workers were grievously injured and six others suffered minor burns. They have been admitted to several government and private hospitals. The police have registered a case on a complaint from the workers. The supervisor of the recycling unit has been named as an accused in the FIR

title: Karnataka Governor seeks authenticated English translations in Zameer prosecution file
content: Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot has sought English translations and authenticated copies of documents submitted by the Lokayukta Police while seeking sanction to prosecute former Minister and senior Congress leader B.Z. Zameer Ahmed Khan in a disproportionate assets case

title: Karnataka High Court defers hearing on KPSC chief’s suspension, seeks clarity on Governor’s power
content: The High Court of Karnataka adjourned till July 15 further hearing on a petition by Shivashankarappa S. Sahukar challenging the legality of the Governor’s order of suspending him as chairperson of the Karnataka Public Service Commission on allegation of misbehaviour

title: UNESCO Award-winning restoration of Thukkachi temple in Tamil Nadu: a blend of traditional wisdom and modern technology
content: On December 6, 2024, UNESCO announced its Award of Distinction, recognising the Thukkachi restoration as an outstanding example of conserving a living heritage monument while preserving its historical authenticity.

title: Delay in opening of restored bridge forces students to cross rail track near Katpadi
content: Residents said that the restored old bridge will help hundreds of residents in at least 10 surrounding farming villages to reach big towns like Katpadi, Gudiyatham, Vellore, and Ranipet

title: Incentives, warnings and assurances: how an Indian sailor transited Hormuz
content: Indian seafarer aboard oil tanker recalls incentives from his employer to make the risky transit via route near Oman, the threats from the Iranian side to stop, and encouragement from the U.S. Navy to move ahead

title: Centre bans paraquat dichloride, a herbicide linked to thousands of self-harm, homicide and accidental deaths
content: India joins the long list of over 70 countries that have banned the toxic herbicide which has no antidote

title: T.N. police mount vigil on six long-distance trains to curb ganja smuggling
content: In July so far, 1,020 drug-related cases have been booked, resulting in the arrest of 1,270 people and recovery of 1,034 kg of ganja, 4.5 kg of other drugs, and 4,579 narcotic tablets

title: Hyderabad native dies in U.S. skydiving accident
content: Massachusetts authorities said a preliminary investigation found that a sudden gust of wind interfered with his parachute during landing.

title: IBM Stocks Slide Most In 58 Years Over CEO Arvind Krishna's "We Faltered On AI" Remark
content: IBM Stock Crash: IBM said its revenue for the three months ending in June rose just one per cent to $17.2 billion.

title: 'Hormuz Control Is A Must': Iran Withdraws From US Deal Over New Blockade
content: Days of retaliatory strikes across the Middle East by Iran — and both nations' attempts to vie for control of the waterway through which a fifth of the world's oil and natural gas passes during peacetime — threaten to push the region back to all-out war.

title: Relief For India, China As US Eases 500% Russian Oil Tariff Threat
content: The updated version also allows an exception for countries that import less than 15 per cent of Russia's natural gas exports and are taking significant steps to reduce those imports, which could exempt Japan, France, Hungary, and Belgium.

title: What Is Pickaxe Mountain And Why Donald Trump Wants To "Take It Out"
content: Pickaxe Mountain is located near Iran's heavily damaged Natanz uranium enrichment facility and is regarded as one of the country's most heavily fortified nuclear-related sites.

title: Centre Expands PM Internship Scheme: How Aspirants, Businesses Will Benefit
content: The Prime Minister Internship Scheme aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore young people over five years in India's leading companies.

title: Passport Is A Document To "Regulate" Departure Of Indians, 8% Have It: Centre
content: A passport is a document that is issued by the government to regulate the departure of citizens from India, the Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Restores Iran Port Blockade Amid Rising Strait Of Hormuz Tensions
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Warm Day In Delhi, Rain Expected From July 14 To 18
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi sees a max temperature of 38.6C with heat index at 45.8C and monsoon rainfall forecast till July 20.

title: Thief Posing As Beggar Steals Rs 1.5 Lakh Phone From Karnataka Shop
content: In a shocking incident in Karnataka's Belagavi, a thief disguised as a beggar allegedly stole a mobile phone worth around Rs 1.5 lakh from a mobile store.

title: 4 Dead, Over 35,000 Affected As Assam Flood Situation Worsens
content: The flood situation in Assam worsened on Tuesday with death count rising to four in the first wave of flood hit the state.

title: "Tremendous Chemistry": Trump Welcomes New Iraqi PM During White House Visit
content: President Donald Trump gave Iraq's new prime minister an effusive welcome at the White House on Tuesday, promoting the "tremendous chemistry" between him and a fellow wealthy businessman.

title: Ex-Columnist Receives $5.63 Million Owed By Trump In Sex Abuse case
content: The writer E. Jean Carroll has collected nearly $5.63 million from Donald Trump after a jury in 2023 found the US president liable for sexually abusing and defaming her, court records show.

title: UP Suspended Municipal Employee Dies By Suicide, Case Filed Against 5
content: According to police, 52-year-old Upendra Shankhdhar was a tax collector suspended in September last year in connection with an alleged Rs 30 lakh tax embezzlement case.

title: Trump Threatens To Hit Iran Power Plants, Bridges Next Week If No Deal
content: President Donald Trump told Fox News on Tuesday he would expand US strikes on Iran next week to target power plants and bridges if Tehran does not make a deal.

title: "Have No Ties To Family": Dhirendra Shastri On Brother's Alleged Firing Case
content: Dhirendra Krishna Shastri, head priest of Bageshwar Dham, said that he has no ties with his estranged brother Shaligram or his family after the latter was accused of allegedly shooting at a person within the

title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 4,734, More Than 16,700 Injured
content: The death toll from the earthquakes that struck Venezuela almost three weeks ago has surpassed 4,700, while almost 17,000 people have been injured, an official report released Tuesday stated.

title: UK To Announce Midnight Social Media Curfew For Teenagers
content: Britain will introduce a default overnight curfew on social media apps for 16- and 17-year-olds, the government said, adding new restrictions for older teenagers to its plan to introduce a sweeping ban on social media for under-16s.

title: Florida Man Killed After Fleeing US Immigration Agents, 3rd Death In A Week
content: A man running from an encounter with immigration and other federal agents in Florida was struck and killed by a tractor trailer on Tuesday, authorities said.

title: US Forces Launch New Round Of Strikes On Iran
content: The United States launched strikes against Iran for the fourth day in a row Tuesday as Washington prepared to reimpose a naval blockade on Iranian ports, the US military said.

title: US Resumes Hormuz Blockade Against Iran, Launches New Strikes
content: Iran War:  The US military said Tuesday that it has reimposed its blockade of Iranian ports in response to Iran's attacks on commercial ships on the Strait of Hormuz, as the interim ceasefire deal unravels and concerns grow about a return to all-out war.

title: ‘If you don’t want E20, you can buy 100% petrol, but pay more’: Nitin Gadkari
content: Union minister Nitin Gadkari denied personal gain from the ethanol blending program. He stated that allegations of conflict of interest are politically motivated and lack substance. Gadkari emphasized his long-standing support for alternative fuels to reduce pollution and import dependence. He clarified that his sons' business has a minimal share in the ethanol sector. The minister also highlighted the program's historical backing from previous governments.

title: 'If she publicly says ...': Cong extends Martyrs' Day invite to Mamata, but with a condition
content: The Congress invited Mamata Banerjee to its Martyrs' Day event in Kolkata. This invitation came with a condition that she acknowledge leaving Congress was a mistake. West Bengal Congress president stated Mamata should not distort the historical significance of the 1993 movement. He added that admitting her past decision was wrong would be a significant gesture. The Congress stage remains open for her to pay respects to the martyrs.

title: Road to SA up and running: Washington, Axar deliver with bat and ball at Edgbaston
content: Axar Patel and Washington Sundar rescued India from 160/4 with an unbeaten 102-run stand to seal a six-wicket win over England in the first ODI at Edgbaston. The partnership not only ended India's winless run in the UK but also highlighted Gautam Gambhir's evolving batting blueprint ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup.

title: One of 'biggest AI problems' is forcing American and European companies to use Chinese models
content: Businesses are replacing costly American AI models with cheaper Chinese options. This shift is driven by escalating usage-based billing for advanced artificial intelligence. Companies like DoorDash and Airbnb now use Chinese AI for routine tasks. European firms also seek alternatives due to US export controls and reliance concerns. Chinese AI models offer comparable efficiency and open-weight accessibility for businesses.

title: Need to relook if English can be considered an indigenous language: SC
content: The Supreme Court questioned English's indigenous status while refusing to halt the CBSE's three-language scheme. This policy aims to promote Hindi and regional languages, aligning with constitutional goals. Petitioners argued against the scheme due to teacher and infrastructure challenges. They also noted limited availability of books on the NCERT website. The Centre and CBSE will file their responses within ten days.

title: 'Won’t quit, Ayodhya Ram temple donation theft due to SBI laxity': Trust treasurer
content: Swami Govinddev Giri Maharaj stated he will not resign as treasurer of the trust. He believes bank officials should have flagged the alleged donation embezzlement. The trust's general secretary Champat Rai recently resigned following the case. Giri Maharaj acknowledged security lapses and promised system strengthening measures. He estimated the embezzlement amount to be around Rs3 crore.

title: Did Lamine Yamal get away with handball? Why Spain's penalty stood against France
content: Spain's 2-0 World Cup semi-final win over France was sparked by a controversial penalty awarded after Lamine Yamal was fouled by Lucas Digne. Claims of a possible handball in the build-up dominated debate, but rules experts explained the contact was not punishable under IFAB laws. Despite Didier Deschamps questioning the officiating, the decision was ultimately deemed correct.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: FBI adds Indian-origin man to most wanted list over alleged organised crime links
content: The FBI has added Nitish Kaushal to its most wanted list for his alleged role in organized crime. Kaushal is linked to the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria group involved in various illegal activities. This action follows charges against Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar for a Canadian assassination. Federal agents recently conducted large raids targeting the Lawrence Bishnoi network in the United States.

title: 1970 Shelby Mustang GT500 sat in barn for 30 years, still has rare V8
content: A rare 1970 Shelby GT500 has resurfaced after spending nearly three decades off the road, offering muscle car enthusiasts a fascinating glimpse into one of Ford's most collectible classics. The car, documented by Ryan Brutt on YouTube's Auto Archaeology channel, has remained largely untouched since it was parked sometime in the 1990s.

title: Princess Kate may be building Diana-style gemstone legacy, expert says
content: Princess Kate impressed royal watchers with three elegant looks during Wimbledon 2026, but it wasn't just her outfits that sparked conversation. According to a jewellery expert, the Princess of Wales' decision to repeatedly wear some of her favourite pieces could be part of a long-term style strategy inspired by her late mother-in-law, Princess Diana.

title: After China sales collapse, Nvidia shuts AI chip 'back door'
content: Nvidia is cutting off over half its Asian customers to stop AI chip smuggling.  The company now physically visits data centers and interviews end-users.  This move follows US pressure and a Supermicro smuggling case.  China faces a severe AI computing power shortage due to export bans.  Beijing hopes domestic factories will triple production capacity by year-end.

title: IBM's 'ugly moment': Worst fall since 1987, $69 bn wiped out — Why did stock crash 25%?
content: ​​Shares of IBM plunged 25% on Tuesday, with the company seeing a steeper one-day decline than it experienced during the 1987 "Black Monday" market crash. The weakness also spread to other software stocks.

title: DHSE Kerala plus one result to be released today: Check complete details here
content: The Directorate of Higher Secondary Education (DHSE), Kerala, is expected to declare the Plus One (Class 11) Result 2026 on July 15. Students can access their scorecards online using their registration number and date of birth. Candidates must secure at least 35 per cent marks to qualify for promotion, while unsuccessful students can appear for the SAY/Improvement examinations.

title: Watch: Riots erupt in France after 0-2 FIFA World Cup semifinal defeat to Spain
content: France's 2026 FIFA World Cup journey concluded with a semifinal loss to Spain. Viral social media claims of widespread riots across France following the defeat . Thousands of disappointed fans gathered in public squares and fan zones after the match. Spain secured a 2-0 victory, ending France's hopes of a third World Cup title. The team's attacking cohesion and Kylian Mbappé's performance were key discussion points.

title: US eases Russia sanctions bill, cuts tariff threat on India and China from 500% to 100%
content: US senators unveiled a revised Russia sanctions bill targeting major energy buyers. This legislation seeks to pressure Moscow and its key oil and gas importers. The updated bill reduces previously proposed tariffs on top purchasers like China and India. It also targets Russia's shadow fleet and financial institutions involved in its war. This measure aims to secure broader political support and honor Senator Lindsey Graham's legacy.

title: NBA trade rumor: Bronny James could rival LeBron James after Lakers move
content: Bronny James remains under a guaranteed contract with the Los Angeles Lakers for the 2026-27 season despite his father LeBron James's confirmed departure from the franchise after eight years. The Athletic's Dan Woike reports the Lakers consider Bronny's roster future independent of LeBron's free agency decision, pointing to his inclusion in the team's veteran minicamp as evidence. While rival executives have discussed Bronny as a potential incentive to help recruit LeBron, no trade has been reported, and the Lakers' own front-office moves suggest they intend to keep developing him.

title: India end England's 12-year home stronghold at Edgbaston with six-wicket win
content: India defeated England by six wickets at Edgbaston on Tuesday, July 14, 2026. England's strong start faltered after reaching sixty-one without loss. Axar Patel's four wickets helped restrict England to two hundred fifty-eight runs. Shubman Gill scored eighty runs, and Axar Patel added fifty-seven not out. India achieved the target with twenty-eight deliveries remaining in the match.

title: Trump pressures Netanyahu to pull Israeli forces out of Syria, Lebanon
content: In a significant move, President Trump has called on Prime Minister Netanyahu to retract Israeli military presence in Syria. However, Netanyahu remains steadfast, emphasizing the necessity of border security due to external threats. Meanwhile, the US is advocating for a mutual security arrangement between Israel and Syria. Escalating incidents in southern Syria and Lebanon have raised regional tensions, while negotiations facilitated by mediators are progressing in Rome.

title: Lamine Yamal's grandmother changed her family's future with one brave journey
content: Lamine Yamal's rise is rooted in a multigenerational immigrant story that began decades before his birth. His paternal grandmother Fátima sneaked onto a bus from Morocco alone, worked three shifts a day, and slowly reunited her family in Mataró. His mother gave birth at 16 and worked fast food to fund his La Masia commute. His father survived a near-fatal stabbing in 2024. Yamal carries all of it, expressed in a simple hand gesture after every goal, three digits that point back to the neighborhood, and the people, that made him.

title: The Ravens and Giants have emerged as favourites for the $69m star receiver
content: The former New England Patriots’ wide receiver Stefon Diggs witnessed a brutal loss in the Super Bowl against the Seattle Seahawks but his performance had impressed many across the league, ahead of the new season. While Stefon Digs dealt with a controversial legal drama, he was released by the New England Patriots just days after the Super Bowl loss. Ahead of the new season, the Baltimore Ravens and the New York Giants have been reported to be eyeing him.

title: Ester Expósito opens up on tough World Cup choice amid Mbappé romance
content: The French star player Kylian Mbappé has been a force to be reckoned with on the field, impressing everyone around the world. While his record breaking performance has impressed billions across the world, his high profile relationship with Spanish actress Ester Expósito has turned heads. As the star player plays against the nation of his girlfriend, Ester Expósito has opened up about it.

title: FIFA World Cup: Yamal shines as Spain outclass Mbappe’s France to reach their second final
content: Spain stormed into their first World Cup final since 2010 with a clinical 2-0 victory over top-ranked France. A first-half penalty from Mikel Oyarzabal, sparked by a smart play from teenage prodigy Lamine Yamal, and a second-half strike by Pedro Porro sealed the win. La Roja will face either Argentina or England in Sunday’s grand finale.

title: Yamal scripts European football history after France win with record no one has ever matched
content: Lamine Yamal extended his perfect record at major international tournaments as Spain's 2-0 World Cup semi-final victory over France made it 12 wins from 12 starts across the FIFA World Cup and UEFA European Championship. No European player has ever maintained a flawless record over as many starts, highlighting the teenager's remarkable impact as Spain prepare for the World Cup final.

title: Oil price today: Crude tops $85 a barrel as US restores Strait of Hormuz blockade
content: Oil prices surged beyond eighty-five dollars per barrel on Wednesday. This occurred after the United States reinstated a naval blockade on Iran. Fighting between Washington and Tehran escalated, raising concerns over crude supplies. The United Arab Emirates reported Iranian cruise missile attacks on oil tankers. American forces continued strikes on Iranian military targets across the region.

title: FIFA WC: Mbappe takes aim at tactics after 'sloppy' France crash out to Spain
content: Kylian Mbappe openly admitted France's World Cup semifinal defeat was a comprehensive failure. He pointed to tactical issues and a lack of effective pressing against Spain's midfield. Mbappe also criticized the team's technical execution and poor first touches after regaining possession. Youngster Rayan Cherki echoed these sentiments, stating France lacked everything on the field. As captain, Mbappe accepted full responsibility for the team's inability to reach the final.

title: Thailand scraps plan to end visa-free entry for Indians
content: Thailand will now allow thirty-day visa-exempt entries for Indian visitors. This decision follows a recent drop in arrivals from India impacting tourism. The government aims to align with Indian tourist travel behavior and boost visitor numbers. Previously, Indian tourists enjoyed sixty days visa-free entry to the country. This change reverses an earlier proposal to eliminate visa-free access for many nations.

title: Bengaluru cop held after wife alleges dowry harassment, machete attack bid on father
content: A Bengaluru police sub-inspector was arrested after his wife alleged dowry harassment and assault. The wife stated that the sub-inspector demanded significant dowry and physically abused her. He also allegedly threatened her and her father with a machete during a dispute. The police initially registered a non-cognisable report before an FIR was filed. The case will now be transferred to another police station for investigation.

title: US resumes Iran naval blockade, launches fourth straight day of strikes over Hormuz
content: The United States has resumed its naval blockade of Iranian ports and coastal areas. American forces also launched additional strikes against Iranian military capabilities linked to shipping attacks. These actions follow a series of strikes and counterstrikes in the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The conflict escalates tensions over one of the world's most important energy transit routes. The US military has significantly expanded its presence across the Middle East.

title: Staines' killer Dara Singh to be released soon?
content: The Supreme Court has set August 19 as a deadline for Odisha to decide on Dara Singh's remission plea. Dara Singh, jailed since 2000, is seeking release after serving over 26 years. The state sentence review board is favorably considering his plea due to good conduct. Updated information is required by the board before a final decision can be made.

title: Quote of the day by Johnny Depp
content: In a surprising turn, Johnny Depp is set to bring Ebenezer Scrooge to life in 'Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol,' releasing in late 2026. This role highlights Depp's ongoing fascination with the complexities of human nature. Alongside this, he stars in the thriller 'Day Drinker' alongside Penélope Cruz. His unique career choices reflect his passion for diving deep into character portrayals with psychological depth.

title: FTA with UK comes into effect today, exports to get a boost
content: The India-UK free trade agreement begins implementation, boosting Indian exports to the UK. Indian service providers and professionals will gain greater access to the United Kingdom market. Many Indian products will enter the UK duty-free, enhancing export competitiveness significantly. Digital service delivery is fully opened, benefiting global capability centers in India. A double contribution convention will save Indian workers and employers substantial annual amounts.

title: Shubman Gill's 80, Axar-Washington stand give India 1-0 lead over England
content: Chasing 259, India lost Rohit Sharma (11) and Virat Kohli (5) early, slipping to 48/2. Gill then rebuilt the innings with vice-captain Shreyas Iyer, adding 101 runs for the third wicket.

title: Aamir Khan faces fatwa after marrying Gauri Spratt; cleric objects to interfaith marriage
content: Aamir Khan has landed in controversy just days after marrying longtime partner Gauri Spratt, with a Muslim cleric objecting to their interfaith marriage and declaring it impermissible under his interpretation of Islamic law. Maulana Ibrahim Hussain, the Shahi Chief Mufti of the Muslim Personal Darul Ifta, has issued a fatwa against Aamir Khan over his marriage to Gauri Spratt, saying it goes against Sharia because she is not a Muslim.

title: Gulf crisis: After fresh attacks, govt orders real-time monitoring of ships with Indian crew
content: India's shipping minister ordered a comprehensive vessel tracking dashboard for ships with Indian seafarers. This dashboard will provide real-time information on vessel position and crew welfare. Liaison officers will be appointed to assist affected seafarers and their families. The ministry also directed close coordination with Indian missions in affected nations. Vessel movements require fresh threat assessments and coordination with maritime authorities.

title: Give Muslims alternative site for namaz: Supreme Court to Dhar administration
content: The Supreme Court will hear challenges to a Madhya Pradesh High Court ruling on the Bhojshala complex. It declined to stay the order which declared the site a temple. Muslim petitioners argued the ruling ignored historical evidence and existing arrangements. The court directed authorities to find an alternative space for Friday prayers. Structural changes to the complex are prohibited without court permission.

title: Passport just a document to regulate departure of citizens from India: MEA
content: The MEA clarified passport issuance follows the Passports Act, 1967. This document regulates departure of Indian citizens from India. Official sources state a passport is not a determinant of citizenship. Less than eight percent of Indians currently hold a passport. This clarification follows recent government official remarks on the travel document.

title: Air India crash: Draft final report on tragedy will be ready only by Oct, AAIB tells SC
content: The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau informed the Supreme Court about the inquiry timeline. The complex investigation into the Air India crash is expected to conclude soon. Analysis activities will be completed within six weeks, subject to external factors. A draft final report is anticipated by October 2026 for review. This timeline aligns with international practices for accident investigations.

title: Vietnam boat survivor: Captain jumped, so did I
content: A survivor described the sudden Vietnam boat tragedy where the captain jumped overboard. He explained that only those who followed him into the water managed to escape. The closed boat prevented rear passengers from exiting, leading to their demise. He lost a childhood friend in the unfortunate incident. Improved island medical access could have saved additional lives.

title: India moves to block imports linked to forced labour amid USTR probe
content: India is inserting a new clause into its foreign trade policy. This clause will allow blocking imports produced using forced labour. The government will use the International Labour Organisation's definition of forced labour. This move strengthens India's legal framework for trade negotiations. The notification becomes effective after thirty days from its issuance.

title: On South China Sea, government backs tribunal ruling against Beijing
content: India supports freedom of navigation and lawful sea uses in the South China Sea. The Ministry of External Affairs responded to a joint statement by several nations. This statement rejected China's expansive South China Sea claim as lacking legal basis. It also reaffirmed a 2016 tribunal ruling favoring the Philippines. India emphasizes peaceful dispute resolution in accordance with UNCLOS principles.

title: PM Modi helped prevent Putin’s tactical nuclear strike on Ukraine in 2022: Polish minister
content: Poland's foreign minister stated Prime Minister Modi influenced President Putin regarding nuclear weapons. He emphasized Modi's unique ability to exert pressure and influence on the Russian leader. India's long-standing relationship with Russia provides a crucial communication channel. India consistently advocates for dialogue and diplomacy to resolve the conflict. The nation maintains ties with both Russia and Western countries.

title: Hydel project near China border gets green clearance
content: An expert panel recommended green clearance for the Naying hydro-electric project. This project will be built across the Siyom river in Arunachal Pradesh. It will boost renewable energy generation and cost an estimated Rs 11,835 crore. The project proponent will provide compensation and rehabilitation for displaced families. Further forest clearance is now required for this significant development.
